fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Jersey Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Jersey - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity new jersey, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to new jersey, speech-language pathologist compact new jersey, new jersey speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology Advisory Committee
board.parent_dept: New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs
board.mailing_address:
State Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology
P.O. Box 45002
Newark, New Jersey 07101

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: Full credential for independent SLP practice in New Jersey.

license_types.title: Temporary SLP Permit
license_types.description: For SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Register and Complete Online Application
steps.description: Visit the New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs' MyLicense portal at https://newjersey.mylicense.com/web/, register as a new user, and complete the comprehensive online application for a Speech-Language Pathologist license.

steps.title: Submit Official Academic Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts, verifying the completion of a master's or doctoral degree in Speech-Language Pathology from an accredited institution, to be sent directly to the Board.

steps.title: Provide Praxis Examination Scores
steps.description: Ensure your official scores from the Praxis examination in Speech-Language Pathology (ETS test code 5331) are sent directly to the Board from ETS.

steps.title: Document Clinical Fellowship Experience
steps.description: Submit detailed documentation of your supervised postgraduate professional experience (Clinical Fellowship Year or its equivalent), including verification from your clinical supervisor, directly to the Board.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Undergo a criminal history background check, which includes fingerprinting, by following the specific instructions provided by the Board after your application has been initiated through the MyLicense portal.

telepractice_text: New Jersey permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. New Jersey is not currently a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: New Jersey issues a Temporary SLP Permit for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: New Jersey does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full New Jersey licensure.

faqs.question: How much does a New Jersey SLP license cost?
Is New Jersey part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does New Jersey allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes, with a valid NJ license.

faqs.question: Does New Jersey have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in New Jersey. To work in New Jersey, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to New Jersey?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 8 weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Jersey while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: New Jersey does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full New Jersey licensure.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in New Jersey
hero.dek: To apply for an initial Speech-Language Pathologist license in New Jersey, users must submit their application and all supporting documents through the New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs' MyLicense portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: To apply, visit the MyLicense portal URL, click 'Apply for a New License' or 'Register as a New User' to create an account with a username and password. Once logged in, select the Speech-Language Pathologist application type to begin the online application.
